items.front: 6-5-1d: Heading divergence required for lateral separation of departing aircraft
6-5-2: GPS crossing restrictions — why reference the next waypoint?
items.back: GPS equipment provides tracking 'TO' data rather than 'FROM' the last waypoint. Issue crossing restrictions in reference to the NEXT waypoint.

items.front: 6-5-2: TBL 6-5-2 vs TBL 6-5-1 — when to use which?
items.back: TBL 6-5-1 = non-DME only. TBL 6-5-2 = DME, compensates for slant-range error with higher values split by altitude. At ≤3,000 ft above NAVAID, TBL 6-5-1 may be used for DME (slant-range negligible).

items.front: 6-5-3: DME arc minima — arc-to-arc vs arc-to-other-airspace
items.back: Arc-to-arc: ≤35 NM → 10 mi; >35 NM → 20 mi. Arc-to-other-airspace: ≤35 NM → 5 mi; >35 NM → 10 mi. Arc-to-other uses half the distance.
VIA (number of miles) MILE ARC (direction) OF (name of DME NAVAID).

items.front: 6-5-4a1: Protected airspace for direct courses via NAVAIDs (≤15° change, FL600 and below)
items.back: 4 mi each side to 51 NM from NAVAID → expands on 4.5° angle → 10 mi each side at 130 NM from NAVAID.

items.front: 6-5-4b/c: Overflown side protection for course changes — 16-90° vs 91-180°
items.back: 16-90°: below FL180 = same as a1; FL180-230 = 14 mi; >FL230 = 17 mi. 91-180°: below FL180 = same as a1; FL180-230 = 28 mi; >FL230 = 34 mi. Note the doubling.

items.front: 6-5-4d: What minima apply after a course change is completed?
items.back: Once the course change is complete and aircraft is back on course, the appropriate direct-course minima in subparagraph a may be used.

items.front: 6-5-5: RNAV diverging/crossing courses — non-overlap distance by route width
items.back: Route ≤8 mi wide → 4 mi beyond non-overlap. Route >8 mi wide → 9 mi. Extended usable distance >130 mi → 15 mi.
